channel.title: Policy & Security Think Tank Updates
channel.description: Custom RSS feed from CSIS, CFR, and other policy institutions

item.title: Russian Crude and India: Here to Stay Amid Middle East Tensions?
item.description: Middle East tensions and threats to Gulf oil supply complicate U.S. pressure on India to phase out Russian crude. Amid uncertainty, Russian crude oil flows to India might endure, not by choice, but by necessity.

item.title: What Does the Iran War Mean for Global Energy Markets?
item.description: In this Experts React, the CSIS Energy Security and Climate Change team evaluates how markets are responding to current events and identifies signposts for escalation or relaxation.

item.title: No One, Not Even Beijing, Is Getting Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As war chokes off traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, Chinese ships are not getting special treatment. New ship-tracking data shows Chinese vessels have largely halted transits, underscoring Beijing’s exposure to instability in a region critical to its energy security.

item.title: The Satellite Encryption Gap: Why Everyday Citizens’ Communications Are at Risk
item.description: Satellites power everything from in-flight Wi-Fi to critical infrastructure communications, yet many systems still transmit data without encryption. This gap leaves sensitive civilian and government communications vulnerable to interception by adversaries.
